# Chroot → running system: the cross-phase bridges 🟡

**Goal.** Build a root filesystem once (Phase 1), then run the SAME artifact as a bootable VM, a rootless OCI image, and an LXD container.

**Audience:** you can build a chroot; now boot it as a VM, image, or container  
**Effort:** ⏱ ~1–2h  
**Prerequisites:** [Containers from first principles](path-container-internals.md)

| # | Lab | You'll learn | Checkpoint (observable) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [`chroot-examples/chroot-debian-bookworm.toml`](../chroot-examples/chroot-debian-bookworm.toml) 🔑 | the source rootfs every bridge starts from. | the chroot builds and enters cleanly. |
| 2 | [`vm-examples/vm-from-chroot-debian.toml`](../vm-examples/vm-from-chroot-debian.toml) | chroot → a bootable BIOS qcow2 (MBR + extlinux + ext4). | the qcow2 boots as a real VM to a login. |
| 3 | [`podman-examples/podman-from-chroot.toml`](../podman-examples/podman-from-chroot.toml) 🪶 | chroot → a rootless OCI image. | `podman run` the imported tree drops you into it. |
| 4 | [`lxd-examples/lxd-from-chroot.toml`](../lxd-examples/lxd-from-chroot.toml) | chroot → an LXD/Incus container image. | `incus launch` the imported image starts it. |
| 5 | [`offsec-awae-vm`](../offsec-awae-vm/) 🔒 | an automated Kali chroot → headless BIOS VM carrying the OffSec AWAE (WEB-300) toolset. | `build-vm.sh --smoke` proves the pipeline; the VM boots with the WEB-300 tools installed. |
| 6 | [`rhel-bootc-minimal`](../rhel-bootc-minimal/) | a *minimal* bootc base image → `bootc install to-disk` → boot it as a real OS. | the image is ~59% smaller than stock; serial login shows an `ostree=` boot of the image's own kernel. |
